original: Mickey Mouse makes his first appearance in the silent film "Plane Crazy"
Richard and Maurice McDonald open the first McDonald's restaurant in San Bernardino, California
Construction begins on the Soviet space travel center at Baikonur in Kazakhstan
USSR launches Sputnik III
Last Project Mercury flight launched, Gordon Cooper in Faith 7
English engineer Frank Whittle successfully tests his design for the first turbojet engine aboard a Gloster E.28/39 flying from RAF Cranwell
Diego Marín Aguilera flies a glider for "about 360 metres", at a height of 5-6 metres, one of the first attempted flights
The Urey-Miller experiment using primordial soup to show how life of earth might have originated, written by Stanley Miller, is published in the journal "Science"
German astronomerJohannes Keplerdiscovers the third of his three planetary laws, the "harmonic law"
First ascent of Makalu, the world's fifth-highest mountain on the China-Nepal border in the Himalayas
Amy Johnsonarrives in Croydon, England having flown from South Africa in a record 4 days and 16 hours
Williamina Fleming, pioneering Scottish astronomer, discovered the Horsehead Nebula (1888), born in Dundee, Scotland (d. 1911)
Maria Reiche, German-Peruvian mathematician and archaeologist specializing in Nazca lines, born in Dresden, Germany (d. 1998)
Pierre Curie, French physicist (Nobel 1903) and husband of Marie Curie, born in Paris, France (d. 1906)
Francis Baily observes "Baily's Beads" during annular solar eclipse
Mike Oldfield, British musician and composer (Tubular Bells), born in Reading, Berkshire
Frank Wilczek, American Physicist (2004 Nobel Prize), born in Mineola, New York
National Woman Suffrage Association forms in New York, founded bySusan B. AnthonyandElizabeth Cady Stanton
First emigrant wagon train to reach California leaves Independence, Missouri, on a 1,730-mile journey over the Sierra Nevada
Record archery score for a pair over 24 hrs, is set
John Smoltz, American Baseball Hall of Fame pitcher (8 × MLB All-Star; World Series 1995; NL Cy Young Award 1996; Atlanta Braves), born in Warren, Michigan
Ellen Church becomes 1st female airline stewardess aboard a United flight from San Francisco to Cheyenne
Moscow Metro opens to public; initial phase was 11 km (6.8 mi) long and included 13 stations
Las Vegas founded in Nevada
Sputnik 4 launched into Earth orbit; later recovery failed
1st Energiya Launch (USSR)
Lyman Gilmore claims to be the first person to fly a powered craft, a steam-powered airplane with a heavy boiler and coal as a power source
1st British turbojet flies
Tornado strikes Jonesboro, Arkansas at 10 PM, killing 36
Jasper Johns, American abstract painter and sculptor (Green Target; Flag), born in Augusta, Georgia[1]

Brian Eno, British rock and ambient musician (Robert Fripp; Roxy Music; Jon Hassell), and record producer (Roxy Music; David Bowie; Talking Heads; U2; Coldplay; Paul Simon), born in Woodbridge, Sussex, England
Laura Hillenbrand, American author (Seabiscuit; Unbroken: A World War II Story of Survival, Resilience, and Redemption), born in Fairfax, Virginia
Ralph Steadman, British illustrator (Hunter S. Thompson books; album cover art), born in Wallasey, Cheshire, England
Wavy Gravy [Hugh Nanton Romney Jr.], American clown entertainer and peace activist, born in East Greenbush, New York
George Harrisonreleases single "All Those Years Ago" in UK: the song was a tribute to John Lennon, and featured Ringo Starr on drums, and Paul and Linda McCartney on backing vocals
TV comedy film "The Harlem Globetrotters onGilligan's Island" airs on NBC
Hollywood landmark the Roosevelt Hotel opens in Los Angeles byDouglas Fairbanks,Mary Pickford, and Sid Grauman, site of the first Academy Awards ceremony in 1929
Salon des Refusés opens in Paris, exhibition of works rejected by official Salon, featuresPaul Cézanne,Camille Pissarro, Henri Fantin-Latour, James Whistler, and Édouard Manet
Buck O'Neilmakes his debut playing for the Negro league baseball team Kansas City Monarchs at Ruppert Stadium, Kansas City
Australia scores 721 runs in one day v Essex, world record
MLB New York Giants pitcher George "Hooks" Wiltse strikes out 4 Reds batters in 1 inning, as catcher dropped a third strike (Palace of the Fans, in Cincinnati, Ohio)
In his first world heavyweight title defense,Rocky MarcianoKOs former championJersey Joe Walcottin the 1st round at Chicago Stadium
Desmond Howard, College Football Hall of Fame wide receiver (Heisman Trophy 1991, U of Michigan; Super Bowl MVP 1996, Green Bay Packers), born in Cleveland, Ohio
Walter "Turk" Broda, Canadian ice hockey goaltender, 5x Stanley Cup, 2x Vezina Trophy (Toronto Maple Leafs, 1935-51), born in Brandon, Manitoba (d. 1972)

Don Nelson, American NBA small forward (Boston Celtics; 5x NBA champion) and coach (Milwaukee Bucks, Dallas Mavericks; 3x Coach of the Year, 2x All-Star Game head coach), named Top 10 Coaches in NBA History, and recipient of the Chuck Daly Lifetime Achievement Award, born in Muskegon, Michigan
Tony England, American scientist and NASA astronaut (STS 51-F), born in Indianapolis, Indiana
Frank L Culbertson Jr, American US Navy officer and NASA astronaut (STS-38), born in Charleston, South Carolina
Mary Lyon, English geneticist (X-chromosome inactivation), born in Norwich, Norfolk (d. 2014)
Frank Evans Heart, American computer engineer (worked on first routing computer for ARPANET, the Internet's predecessor), born in the Bronx, New York (d. 2018)
Ilya Mechnikov, Russian zoologist and bacteriologist known as the "father of natural immunity" (Nobel 1908), born in Ivanovka, Kharkov Governorate, Russian Empire (d. 1916)
Maria Theresia von Paradis, Austrian blind pianist, singer, organist, and composer, born in Vienna, Austrian Empire (d. 1824)

Ellis Larkins, American classically trained (1st Black student at Peabody Institute) jazz pianist (Ruby Braff), and accompanist (Ella Fitzgerald; Eartha Kitt, Mildred Bailey), born in Baltimore Maryland (d. 2002)
(Bruce) "Utah" Phillips, American folk singer-songwriter ("Green Rolling Hills"; "The Goodnight-Loving Trail") storyteller, poet, and labor organizer, born in Cleveland, Ohio (d. 2008)
Melle Mel [Melvin Glover], American rapper (Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five), born in The Bronx, New York
Prince Be [Attrell Cordes], American rapper (PM Dawn), born in Jersey City (d. 2016)
Freddie Perren, American songwriter and record producer (The Sylvers - "Boogie Fever"; Gloria Gaynor - "I Will Survive"; Peaches & Herb - "Reunited"), born in Englewood, New Jersey  (d. 2004)
Assala [Nasri], Syrian-Bahraini Middle Eastern and Arabic pop singer ("Ma Aaaraftik"), born in Damascus, Syria
Eddy Arnold, American country music singer ("Make The World Go Away"; "Then You Can Tell Me Goodbye"), born in Henderson Tennessee (d. 2008)
(Trinidad) "Trini" Lopez, American singer and guitarist ("If I Had A Hammer"; "Lemon Tree"), and actor (The Dirty Dozen), born in Dallas, Texas (d. 2020)[1]
Paul Zindel, American playwright and novelist (The Effect of Gamma Rays on Man-in-theMoon Marigolds), born in Staten Island, New York (d. 2003)
